tldr: Replace heavyweight Lottie animations with simple static Feather SVG icons where motion is not essential — a scope reduction more than a like-for-like swap.
why_switch: Static SVG icons load instantly with zero runtime cost
Removes dependency on Lottie player libraries and JSON assets
MIT-licensed minimal aesthetic fits developer-facing UIs
Easier to make accessible than animated graphics
what_to_export: Inventory of every Lottie animation in the product and where it renders
Purpose tag for each animation (decorative, status, loading, illustration)
Source .json files and the originating After Effects compositions
Branding/color tokens applied to the animations
List of animations you actually want to keep as motion vs replace with static
export_method: Audit each Lottie usage and classify whether motion is load-bearing. For the ones that aren't, choose a Feather icon that conveys the same meaning. Export the JSON files from your LottieFiles workspace via its official UI for archival. Keep a spreadsheet of replacements with before/after screenshots so design can sign off.
import_method: Install Feather via its official package (e.g. feather-icons or react-feather per docs) and replace each Lottie component with the chosen <FeatherIcon /> at the same size and color. Delete unused Lottie JSON files and the player dependency once nothing references them. For animations you must retain (e.g. critical loaders), keep Lottie isolated to that surface rather than removing it wholesale.

concept_mapping.note: Pair with a CSS keyframe rotation for movement.
Use a brief CSS transition for entrance instead of full animation.
Keep illustration as Lottie/SVG/raster — Feather only covers UI icons.
Coverage is much smaller; consult Feather's official catalog.
Re-implement movement via CSS or framework primitives.
Static icons inherently respect motion preferences.

gotchas: Feather Icons is not a Lottie replacement — only swap where motion isn't essential
Feather has ~280 icons; some Lottie concepts won't have a clean equivalent
Marketing illustrations and complex motion will still need Lottie or another tool
Feather maintenance has been slow; consider Lucide if you want active updates
Removing Lottie player from the bundle requires verifying no leftover imports
Get design sign-off on each replacement to avoid silent UX regressions

email.subject: Trimming Lottie use in favor of static Feather icons
email.body:
Hey team,

I want to audit our LottieFiles usage and replace decorative animations with static Feather Icons where motion isn't load-bearing. The goal is faster loads, smaller bundle, and simpler accessibility — not removing Lottie entirely.

My plan: build a spreadsheet of every Lottie surface, mark each as 'keep' or 'replace', and pair the replace items with a Feather equivalent. Anything decorative becomes a Feather SVG; critical motion (e.g. brand illustrations) stays on Lottie. Estimated effort: 3-10 hours.

Flag any animations you consider untouchable before I start, please.

Thanks
